heading: Covered countermeasure process
label: 247d–6e Covered countermeasure process
notes: References in TextH. Con. Res. 95 of the 109th Congress, referred to in subsec. (a), is H. Con. Res. 95, , , which is not classified to the Code.Apr. 28, 2005119 Stat. 3633
amendments: References in TextH. Con. Res. 95 of the 109th Congress, referred to in subsec. (a), is H. Con. Res. 95, , , which is not classified to the Code.Apr. 28, 2005119 Stat. 3633

children.heading: Establishment of Fund
children.label: a Establishment of Fund
children.text: Upon the issuance by the Secretary of a declaration under , there is hereby established in the Treasury an emergency fund designated as the “Covered Countermeasure Process Fund” for purposes of providing timely, uniform, and adequate compensation to eligible individuals for covered injuries directly caused by the administration or use of a covered countermeasure pursuant to such declaration, which Fund shall consist of such amounts designated as emergency appropriations under section 402 of H. Con. Res. 95 of the 109th Congress, this emergency designation shall remain in effect through .section 247d–6d(b) of this titleOctober 1, 2006

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the Secretary issues a declaration under 247d–6d(b) of this title, the Secretary shall, after amounts have by law been provided for the Fund under subsection (a), provide compensation to an eligible individual for a covered injury directly caused by the administration or use of a covered countermeasure pursuant to such declaration.

children.heading: Elements of compensation
children.label: 2 Elements of compensation
children.text: The compensation that shall be provided pursuant to paragraph (1) shall have the same elements, and be in the same amount, as is prescribed by sections 239c, 239d, and 239e of this title in the case of certain individuals injured as a result of administration of certain countermeasures against smallpox, except that shall not apply.section 239e(a)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: Neither reasonable and necessary medical benefits nor lifetime total benefits for lost employment income due to permanent and total disability shall be limited by .section 239e of this title

children.heading: Determination of eligibility and compensation
children.label: 4 Determination of eligibility and compensation
children.text: Except as provided in this section, the procedures for determining, and for reviewing a determination of, whether an individual is an eligible individual, whether such individual has sustained a covered injury, whether compensation may be available under this section, and the amount of such compensation shall be those stated in (other than in subsection (d)(2) of such section), in regulations issued pursuant to that section, and in such additional or alternate regulations as the Secretary may promulgate for purposes of this section. In making determinations under this section, other than those described in paragraph (5)(A) as to the direct causation of a covered injury, the Secretary may only make such determination based on compelling, reliable, valid, medical and scientific evidence.section 239a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall by regulation establish a table identifying covered injuries that shall be presumed to be directly caused by the administration or use of a covered countermeasure and the time period in which the first symptom or manifestation of onset of each such adverse effect must manifest in order for such presumption to apply. The Secretary may only identify such covered injuries, for purpose of inclusion on the table, where the Secretary determines, based on compelling, reliable, valid, medical and scientific evidence that administration or use of the covered countermeasure directly caused such covered injury.

children.heading: Amendments
children.label: B Amendments
children.text: The provisions of (other than a provision of subsection (a)(2) of such section that relates to accidental vaccinia inoculation) shall apply to the table established under this section.section 239b of this title

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: C Judicial review
children.text: No court of the United States, or of any State, shall have subject matter jurisdiction to review, whether by mandamus or otherwise, any action by the Secretary under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: the terms “vaccine” and “smallpox vaccine” shall be deemed to mean a covered countermeasure;

children.label: B
children.text: the terms “smallpox vaccine injury table” and “table established under ” shall be deemed to refer to the table established under paragraph (4); andsection 239b of this title

children.label: C
children.text: other terms used in those sections shall have the meanings given to such terms by this section.

children.heading: Voluntary program
children.label: c Voluntary program
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that a State, local, or Department of Health and Human Services plan to administer or use a covered countermeasure is consistent with any declaration under 247d–6d of this title and any applicable guidelines of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and that potential participants are educated with respect to contraindications, the voluntary nature of the program, and the availability of potential benefits and compensation under this part.

children.heading: Exhaustion
children.label: 1 Exhaustion
children.text: Subject to paragraph (5), a covered individual may not bring a civil action under against a covered person (as such term is defined in ) unless such individual has exhausted such remedies as are available under subsection (a), except that if amounts have not by law been provided for the Fund under subsection (a), or if the Secretary fails to make a final determination on a request for benefits or compensation filed in accordance with the requirements of this section within 240 days after such request was filed, the individual may seek any remedy that may be available under .section 247d–6d(d) of this titlesection 247d–6d(i)(2) of this titlesection 247d–6d(d) of this title

children.heading: Tolling of statute of limitations
children.label: 2 Tolling of statute of limitations
children.text: The time limit for filing a civil action under for an injury or death shall be tolled during the pendency of a claim for compensation under subsection (a).section 247d–6d(d) of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: This section shall not be construed as superseding or otherwise affecting the application of a requirement, under chapter 171 of title 28, to exhaust administrative remedies.

children.heading: Exclusivity
children.label: 4 Exclusivity
children.text: The remedy provided by subsection (a) shall be exclusive of any other civil action or proceeding for any claim or suit this section encompasses, except for a proceeding under .section 247d–6d of this title

children.heading: Election
children.label: 5 Election
children.text: If under subsection (a) the Secretary determines that a covered individual qualifies for compensation, the individual has an election to accept the compensation or to bring an action under . If such individual elects to accept the compensation, the individual may not bring such an action.section 247d–6d(d) of this title

children.heading: Covered countermeasure
children.label: 1 Covered countermeasure
children.text: The term “covered countermeasure” has the meaning given such term in .section 247d–6d of this title

children.label: A
children.text: who is in a population specified in such declaration, and with respect to whom the administration or use of the covered countermeasure satisfies the other specifications of such declaration; or

children.label: B
children.text: who uses the covered countermeasure, or to whom the covered countermeasure is administered, in a good faith belief that the individual is in the category described by subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Covered injury
children.label: 3 Covered injury
children.text: The term “covered injury” means serious physical injury or death.

children.heading: Declaration
children.label: 4 Declaration
children.text: The term “declaration” means a declaration under .section 247d–6d(b) of this title

children.heading: Eligible individual
children.label: 5 Eligible individual
children.text: The term “eligible individual” means an individual who is determined, in accordance with subsection (b), to be a covered individual who sustains a covered injury.
